items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
# *Indian Standard*
items.value: Indian Standard

items.md: # REQUIREMENTS FOR CHLORINATION EQUIPMENT
items.value: REQUIREMENTS FOR CHLORINATION EQUIPMENT

items.md: ## PART 5 BLEACHING POWDER SOLUTION FEEDER DISPLACEMENT TYPE CHLORINATOR
items.value: PART 5 BLEACHING POWDER SOLUTION FEEDER DISPLACEMENT TYPE CHLORINATOR

items.md: ### 0. FOREWORD
items.value: 0. FOREWORD

items.md: **0.1** This Indian Standard (Part 5) was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards on 30 April 1987, after the draft finalized by the Public Health Engineering Equipment Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.
items.value: 0.1 This Indian Standard (Part 5) was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards on 30 April 1987, after the draft finalized by the Public Health Engineering Equipment Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.

items.md: **0.2** Bleaching powder solution type chlorinators are generally used in water treatment plants having capacities up to 1 MLD and in rural water supplies for disinfection of drinking water. This standard is intended to cover essential features of bleaching powder solution feeder displacement type chlorinator.
items.value: 0.2 Bleaching powder solution type chlorinators are generally used in water treatment plants having capacities up to 1 MLD and in rural water supplies for disinfection of drinking water. This standard is intended to cover essential features of bleaching powder solution feeder displacement type chlorinator.

items.md: **0.3** Bleaching powder is a variable mixture of calcium hydroxide, calcium chloride and calcium hypochlorite. When mixed with water, calcium hypochlorite dissociates into calcium hydroxide and hypochlorous acid (HOCl). Bleaching powder is made into a thin slurry with water and the supernatent which contains chlorine in solution is applied to water using a differential pressure dosing equipment.
items.value: 0.3 Bleaching powder is a variable mixture of calcium hydroxide, calcium chloride and calcium hypochlorite. When mixed with water, calcium hypochlorite dissociates into calcium hydroxide and hypochlorous acid (HOCl). Bleaching powder is made into a thin slurry with water and the supernatent which contains chlorine in solution is applied to water using a differential pressure dosing equipment.

items.md: **0.4** For the purpose of deciding whether a particular requirement of this standard is complied with, the final value, observed or calculated, expressing the result of a test or analysis, shall be rounded off in accordance with IS : 2-1960\*. The number of significant places retained in the rounded off value should be the same as that of the specified value in this standard.
items.value: 0.4 For the purpose of deciding whether a particular requirement of this standard is complied with, the final value, observed or calculated, expressing the result of a test or analysis, shall be rounded off in accordance with IS : 2-1960*. The number of significant places retained in the rounded off value should be the same as that of the specified value in this standard.

items.md: ### 1. SCOPE
items.value: 1. SCOPE

items.md: **1.1** This standard ( Part 5 ) lays down requirements for sizes, material and construction of bleaching powder solution feeder displacement type chlorinators.
items.value: 1.1 This standard ( Part 5 ) lays down requirements for sizes, material and construction of bleaching powder solution feeder displacement type chlorinators.

items.md: \*Rules for rounding off numerical values (*revised*).
items.value: *Rules for rounding off numerical values (revised).

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
### 2. CHLORINATOR CONSTRUCTION
items.value: 2. CHLORINATOR CONSTRUCTION

items.md: **2.1** The displacement type chlorinator consists of:
items.value: 2.1 The displacement type chlorinator consists of:

items.md: a) A fabricated mild steel pressure vessel with a removable flanged cover, drain and outlet valves and flexible plastic or rubber bag secured tight at its neck into which the bleaching powder solution is filled and from which it is fed to the pipeline carrying water to be chlorinated. The pressure vessel is designed for a working pressure of 0.175 N/mm<sup>2</sup> and is tested for 0.35 N/mm<sup>2</sup>;
items.value: a) A fabricated mild steel pressure vessel with a removable flanged cover, drain and outlet valves and flexible plastic or rubber bag secured tight at its neck into which the bleaching powder solution is filled and from which it is fed to the pipeline carrying water to be chlorinated. The pressure vessel is designed for a working pressure of 0.175 N/mm<sup>2</sup> and is tested for 0.35 N/mm<sup>2</sup>;

items.md: b) An orifice plate or venturi tube to produce a differential pressure in the pipeline. The differential pressure normally employed is 2 m of water head at maximum flow but can be varied, if necessary, to suit specific requirements;
items.value: b) An orifice plate or venturi tube to produce a differential pressure in the pipeline. The differential pressure normally employed is 2 m of water head at maximum flow but can be varied, if necessary, to suit specific requirements;

items.md: c) Withdrawal injection fitting with isolating valve for insertion in the pipeline with necessary tubing to connect it with the pressure vessel. The fittings are to withstand the pressure within the doser;
items.value: c) Withdrawal injection fitting with isolating valve for insertion in the pipeline with necessary tubing to connect it with the pressure vessel. The fittings are to withstand the pressure within the doser;

items.md: d) A dose indicator and regulating valve attached to the pressure vessel; and
items.value: d) A dose indicator and regulating valve attached to the pressure vessel; and

items.md: e) A solution preparing tank with necessary valves and pipings to enable the solution to flow by gravity into the pressure vessel.
items.value: e) A solution preparing tank with necessary valves and pipings to enable the solution to flow by gravity into the pressure vessel.

items.md: A typical sketch of the assembly is shown in Fig. 1.
items.value: A typical sketch of the assembly is shown in Fig. 1.

items.md: ### 3. MATERIALS
items.value: 3. MATERIALS

items.md: **3.1** Materials to be used in different components are given in Table 1.
items.value: 3.1 Materials to be used in different components are given in Table 1.

items.md: ### 4. SIZES
items.value: 4. SIZES

items.md: **4.1** The size of the chlorinator is defined based on the capacity of the plastic or rubber bags. The chlorinators are of the following sizes:
items.value: 4.1 The size of the chlorinator is defined based on the capacity of the plastic or rubber bags. The chlorinators are of the following sizes:

items.md: ### 5. SELECTION OF DOSER SIZE
items.value: 5. SELECTION OF DOSER SIZE

items.md: **5.1** Capacity of doser is selected based on volume of bleaching powder solution required per day. Required volume of bleaching powder solution is calculated using the following formula:
items.value: 5.1 Capacity of doser is selected based on volume of bleaching powder solution required per day. Required volume of bleaching powder solution is calculated using the following formula:

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
### ORIFICE ASSEMBLY
items.value: ORIFICE ASSEMBLY

items.md: The image shows a technical diagram of a Bleaching Powder Solution Feeder (Displacement Type Chlorinator).
items.value: The image shows a technical diagram of a Bleaching Powder Solution Feeder (Displacement Type Chlorinator).

items.md: **Components labeled in the diagram:**
items.value: Components labeled in the diagram:

items.md:
* **ORIFICE ASSEMBLY**: Located on the main pipeline.
* **CHARGING DEVICE WHEEL**: At the top of the doser.
* **SPINDLE CLAMP**: Securing the top assembly.
* **FEED CHECK DEVICE**: Connected to the solution outlet line.
* **SOLUTION OUTLET VALVE**: Controls flow from the doser to the main line.
* **INLET FLOW VALVE**: Controls flow from the main line to the doser.
* **SOLUTION INLET VALVE (WHERE SOLUTIONING TANK PROVIDED)**: Side inlet to the doser neck.
* **FLANGE DETAIL 'A'**: Connection point below the spindle clamp.
* **CHARGING HOOD**: Top section of the doser.
* **DOSER NECK**: Narrow section above the main tank.
* **AIR RELEASE VALVE**: Located on the upper side of the tank.
* **DOSER WITH RUBBER BAG**: The main cylindrical tank body.
* **MS SUPPORTING STAND**: Legs supporting the tank.
* **DRAIN VALVE**: Located at the bottom of the tank.
**ORIFICE ASSEMBLY**: Located on the main pipeline.
